Enrollment Grand Totals (annual full-paying equivalent or FPE††) †Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) is the percentage equivalent to full-time work; 1.0 FTE equals 100% of full time, 0.5 FTE equals 50% or half time. FTE can be used to represent percentage of faculty time teaching, or time spent as a student. For example, a faculty member may teach three-fifths of the time, or 0.60 FTE; a quarter-time student would be 0.25 FTE. ††Full Paying Equivalent (FPE) is derived from dividing total tuition dollars collected by the full-time tuition rate. Tuition dollars collected include cash, loan, and financial aid monies used to pay tuition. A 1,230 FPE represents tuition for 1,230 full-paying students, though this may consist of some part-time and full-paying students. *Fall only, not annual **Starting fall 2013 students studying off-campus are not included in the enrollment totals. Reed College | Data USA The total enrollment at Reed College in 2022, both undergraduate and graduate, is 1,534 students. The full-time enrollment at Reed College is 1,492 and the part ... In 2022, the undergraduate acceptance rate of Reed College was 30.8% (2,779 admissions from 9,023 applications). This is lower than the acceptance rate of 2021, which was 43.8%. Between 2021 and 2022, the number of applicants grew by 28.7%, while admissions declined by 9.51%. This chart compares the acceptance rate of Reed College (in red) with that of other similar universities, and the chart below shows the acceptance rate by gender. Reed College had a total enrollment of 1,534 students in 2022. The full-time enrollment at Reed College is 1,492 students and the part-time enrollment is 42. This means that 97.3% of students enrolled at Reed College are enrolled full-time. The enrolled student population at Reed College, both undergraduate and graduate, is 60% White, 11.8% Hispanic or Latino, 9.84% Two or More Races, 7.5% Asian, 1.04% Black or African American, and 0.196% American Indian or Alaska Native. Students enrolled at Reed College in full-time Undergraduate programs are most commonly White Female (33.7%), followed by White Male (26.3%) and Hispanic or Latino Female (7.91%). The total enrollment at Reed College in 2022, both undergraduate and graduate, is 1,534 students. The full-time enrollment at Reed College is 1,492 and the part-time enrollment is 42. This means that 97.3% of students enrolled at Reed College are enrolled full-time compared with 79.6% at similar Baccalaureate Colleges. This chart shows the full-time vs part-time enrollment status at Reed College (in red) compares to similar universities.

Reed College - Wikipedia Reed College is a private liberal arts college in Portland, Oregon, United States. Founded in 1908, Reed is a residential college with a campus in the ... Reed is singled out as having little to no grade inflation over the years;[24] only ten students graduated with a perfect 4.0 GPA in the period from 1983 to 2012.[25] (Transcripts are accompanied by a card contextualizing Reed's grading approach so as not to penalize students' graduate school applications.)[26] Although Reed does not award Latin honors to graduates, it confers several awards for academic achievement at commencement, including naming students to Phi Beta Kappa.[27] Academics Reed categorizes its academic program into five Divisions and the Humanities program. Overall, Reed offers five Humanities courses, twenty-six department majors, twelve interdisciplinary majors, six dual-degree programs with other colleges and universities, and programs for pre-medical and pre-veterinary students. Its three most popular majors, based on 2023 graduates, were Psychology, Biology/Biological Sciences, and Computer and Information Sciences.[34] Divisions Division of Arts: includes the Art (Art History and Studio Art), Dance, Music, and Theatre Departments; Division of History and Social Sciences: includes the History, Anthropology, Economics, Political Science, and Sociology Departments, as well as the International and Comparative Policy Studies Program; Division of Literature and Languages: includes the Classics, Chinese, English, French, German, Russian, and Spanish Departments, as well as the Creative Writing and General Literature Programs; Division of Mathematics and Natural Sciences: includes the Mathematics, Biology, Chemistry, and Physics Departments, and Division of Philosophy, Religion, Psychology, and Linguistics: includes the Philosophy, Religion, Psychology, and Linguistics Departments.
